draft.introduction: Psalm 121 is a Song of Ascents, sung by pilgrims traveling to Jerusalem. Its assurance that the Lord “who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep” has made it one of the most comforting psalms in the tradition.

translation.text: I lift up my eyes to the hills. From where does my help come? My help comes from the Lord, who made heaven and earth. He will not let your foot be moved; he who keeps you will not slumber. The Lord will keep your going out and your coming in from this time forth and forevermore.
translation.original_text: ἦρα τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς μου εἰς τὰ ὄρη πόθεν ἥξει ἡ βοήθειά μου

translator_notes.note: The “hills” may refer to the hills surrounding Jerusalem, but some scholars see an ironic allusion to Canaanite high places where idolatry was practiced. The question “from where?” is rhetorical: help comes not from the hills but from the Lord who made them.
